note: GENERATED by gen_tables.py from discovery_OURS-NYC.json. DO NOT HAND-EDIT: this file was hand-maintained once, silently went stale, and the verifier trusted it as ground truth.

candidate_reranking_50.alpha_sweep_note: Fusing the 7B model's scores with the counter at ANY weight alpha>0 lowers Acc@10 monotonically from the counter's 0.6394 to the LLM's 0.3909. The accuracy-optimal fusion weight is exactly zero.

headline: 79.0% of the benchmark is return prediction. On the 21.0% that are discoveries, a global popularity baseline (0.0455) beats the graph transformer (0.0137) and a counter (0.0043). A counter is very hard to beat on the aggregate metric, but it is NOT 'the optimal solution': it loses at Acc@1. That earlier phrasing is retracted.
mechanism: GETNext's transformer performs no sequence modelling: TransformerEncoderLayer is built without batch_first (expects seq,batch,dim) while the input is pad_sequence(batch_first=True) -> (batch,seq,dim), and the causal mask is (batch,batch) -- the exact shape PyTorch validates, so nothing raises. Verified in isolation: perturbing one user's trajectory moves other users' outputs by 2.21, while perturbing a trajectory's own earlier check-in moves its later one by 0.000000. GETNext is therefore in the same model class as a counter, which is why a counter matches it.
scope: We report what the released artifacts do. We make no claim about which code revision produced the published numbers.
caveats: NYC only so far; TKY pending (GETNext-TKY paused for GPU).
LLM arm is single-seed and has real variance: two runs of the identical LoRA configuration gave Acc@10 .4222 and .3909 (7.4% relative). Both are far below COUNT.
Our own split has a 4-7% boundary overlap (check_leakage.py); a strictly clean control is pending.
